Administrative & Regulatory Processes.label: Eligibility Determination
Administrative & Regulatory Processes.definition: The process of assessing whether an individual or family meets the criteria for a program or benefit.

Administrative & Regulatory Processes.label: Enrollment
Administrative & Regulatory Processes.definition: The formal process of registering an individual or family for a program or service.

Administrative & Regulatory Processes.label: Notice and Comment
Administrative & Regulatory Processes.definition: A public rulemaking process where a proposed rule is published and the public is invited to comment.

Administrative & Regulatory Processes.label: Grant Funding
Administrative & Regulatory Processes.definition: The financial assistance awarded by a government entity to a recipient to carry out a public purpose.

Administrative & Regulatory Processes.label: Immigration Status Verification
Administrative & Regulatory Processes.definition: The process of confirming an individual's legal status in the country for eligibility purposes.

Administrative & Regulatory Processes.label: Uncompensated Care
Administrative & Regulatory Processes.definition: Healthcare services provided by hospitals or other providers that do not receive reimbursement.

Core Concepts & Principles.label: Public Health
Core Concepts & Principles.definition: The science and art of preventing disease, prolonging life, and promoting health through organized community efforts.

Core Concepts & Principles.label: Federal Public Benefit
Core Concepts & Principles.definition: A specific set of federally funded benefits defined under PRWORA, generally restricted to qualified aliens.

Core Concepts & Principles.label: Safety Net
Core Concepts & Principles.definition: A collection of services provided by the state or other institutions to prevent individuals from falling into poverty or crisis.

Core Concepts & Principles.label: Administrative Burden
Core Concepts & Principles.definition: The time, effort, and costs incurred by individuals and providers to comply with government regulations.

Core Concepts & Principles.label: Chilling Effect
Core Concepts & Principles.definition: The inhibition or discouragement of the legitimate exercise of a right by the threat of legal sanction or other negative consequences.

Core Concepts & Principles.label: Health Equity
Core Concepts & Principles.definition: The principle that everyone has a fair and just opportunity to be as healthy as possible.

Federal Programs & Grants.label: Head Start
Federal Programs & Grants.definition: A federal program providing comprehensive early childhood education, health, nutrition, and parent involvement services to low-income children and families.

Federal Programs & Grants.label: Health Center Program
Federal Programs & Grants.definition: A federal grant program under Section 330 of the PHS Act funding Community Health Centers to serve medically underserved populations.

Federal Programs & Grants.label: Title X Program
Federal Programs & Grants.definition: The only federal grant program dedicated solely to providing individuals with comprehensive family planning and related preventive health services.

Federal Programs & Grants.label: Early Head Start
Federal Programs & Grants.definition: A federal program serving infants, toddlers, and pregnant women from low-income families to promote healthy prenatal outcomes and development.

Federal Programs & Grants.label: SUBG
Federal Programs & Grants.definition: The Substance Use Prevention, Treatment, and Recovery Services Block Grant, supporting state substance use disorder systems.
terms: Substance Use Prevention, Treatment, and Recovery Services Block Grant

Federal Programs & Grants.label: CSBG
Federal Programs & Grants.definition: The Community Services Block Grant, a federal anti-poverty block grant for services like housing, nutrition, and employment.

Federal Programs & Grants.label: CMHS Block Grant
Federal Programs & Grants.definition: The Community Mental Health Services Block Grant, funding services for adults with serious mental illnesses and children with serious emotional disturbances.
terms: Community Mental Health Services Block Grant

Federal Programs & Grants.label: PATH Grant Program
Federal Programs & Grants.definition: The Projects for Assistance in Transition from Homelessness grant program, funding services for individuals with serious mental illness experiencing homelessness.
terms: Projects for Assistance in Transition from Homelessness Grant Program

Federal Programs & Grants.label: Title IV-E Prevention
Federal Programs & Grants.definition: A federal program providing services for mental health, substance abuse, and in-home parenting skills to prevent foster care entry.

Federal Programs & Grants.label: Migrant Head Start
Federal Programs & Grants.definition: A federal program providing Head Start services to children of migrant and seasonal farmworker families.

Government Agencies & Bodies.label: HHS
Government Agencies & Bodies.definition: The U.S. Department of Health & Human Services, a federal agency protecting American health and providing essential human services.
terms: Department of Health and Human Services
US Department of Health and Human Services

Government Agencies & Bodies.label: Congress
Government Agencies & Bodies.definition: The bicameral legislature of the federal government of the United States.

Government Agencies & Bodies.label: DOJ
Government Agencies & Bodies.definition: The U.S. Department of Justice, the federal executive department responsible for the enforcement of law and administration of justice.

Government Agencies & Bodies.label: SAMHSA
Government Agencies & Bodies.definition: The Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration, an agency of HHS leading public health efforts for mental health.
terms: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration

Government Agencies & Bodies.label: HRSA
Government Agencies & Bodies.definition: The Health Resources and Services Administration, an agency of HHS improving health care for geographically isolated or vulnerable populations.

Government Agencies & Bodies.label: Supreme Court
Government Agencies & Bodies.definition: The highest court in the federal judiciary of the United States of America.

Government Agencies & Bodies.label: ACF
Government Agencies & Bodies.definition: The Administration for Children and Families, a division of HHS promoting the economic and social well-being of families and children.

Government Agencies & Bodies.label: ICE
Government Agencies & Bodies.definition: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement, a federal law enforcement agency under the Department of Homeland Security.

Health Conditions & Diseases.label: Chronic Disease
Health Conditions & Diseases.definition: A condition that lasts for a long duration, generally one year or more, and requires ongoing medical attention.

Health Conditions & Diseases.label: Communicable Disease
Health Conditions & Diseases.definition: An infectious disease transmissible from one person to another by direct or indirect contact.

Health Conditions & Diseases.label: Substance Use Disorder
Health Conditions & Diseases.definition: A disease that affects a person's brain and behavior, leading to an inability to control the use of a substance.

Health Conditions & Diseases.label: STI
Health Conditions & Diseases.definition: A sexually transmitted infection passed from one person to another through sexual contact.

Health Conditions & Diseases.label: HIV/AIDS
Health Conditions & Diseases.definition: Human immunodeficiency virus infection and acquired immunodeficiency syndrome, a spectrum of conditions caused by infection with HIV.

Health Conditions & Diseases.label: Mental Illness
Health Conditions & Diseases.definition: A wide range of mental health conditions that affect mood, thinking, and behavior.

Health Conditions & Diseases.label: COVID-19
Health Conditions & Diseases.definition: A contagious disease caused by a virus named severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2).

Healthcare Programs & Insurance.label: Medicaid
Healthcare Programs & Insurance.definition: A joint federal and state program that provides health coverage to millions of low-income Americans.

Healthcare Programs & Insurance.label: Medicare
Healthcare Programs & Insurance.definition: A federal health insurance program for people who are 65 or older and certain younger people with disabilities.

Healthcare Programs & Insurance.label: SNAP
Healthcare Programs & Insurance.definition: The Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program, a federal program that provides food-purchasing assistance for low-income people.

Healthcare Programs & Insurance.label: TANF
Healthcare Programs & Insurance.definition: The Temporary Assistance for Needy Families program, providing temporary financial assistance for pregnant women and families with dependent children.

Healthcare Programs & Insurance.label: CHIP
Healthcare Programs & Insurance.definition: The Children's Health Insurance Program, providing low-cost health coverage to children in families who earn too much for Medicaid.

Healthcare Services & Delivery Models.label: Community Health Center
Healthcare Services & Delivery Models.definition: A community-based clinic that provides primary care services in underserved areas, regardless of ability to pay.

Healthcare Services & Delivery Models.label: FQHC
Healthcare Services & Delivery Models.definition: A Federally Qualified Health Center, a type of provider that receives federal grants under Section 330 of the PHS Act.

Healthcare Services & Delivery Models.label: Preventive Care
Healthcare Services & Delivery Models.definition: Routine health care that includes screenings, check-ups, and patient counseling to prevent illnesses, disease, or other health problems.

Healthcare Services & Delivery Models.label: Primary Care
Healthcare Services & Delivery Models.definition: Health services that cover a range of prevention, wellness, and treatment for common illnesses.

Healthcare Services & Delivery Models.label: Emergency Care
Healthcare Services & Delivery Models.definition: Medical services provided for a condition that could lead to serious disability or death if not immediately treated.

Healthcare Services & Delivery Models.label: Behavioral Health Services
Healthcare Services & Delivery Models.definition: Services for mental health conditions, substance use disorders, life stressors, and crises.

Healthcare Services & Delivery Models.label: Vaccinations
Healthcare Services & Delivery Models.definition: Treatment with a vaccine to produce immunity against a disease; inoculation.

Healthcare Services & Delivery Models.label: Prenatal Care
Healthcare Services & Delivery Models.definition: Health care for pregnant individuals before birth, involving treatments and trainings to ensure a healthy pregnancy, labor, and delivery.

Healthcare Services & Delivery Models.label: Family Planning
Healthcare Services & Delivery Models.definition: Services that help individuals and couples decide if and when to have children, including contraception and counseling.

Healthcare Services & Delivery Models.label: Substance Use Treatment
Healthcare Services & Delivery Models.definition: Medical and psychotherapeutic treatment for dependency on psychoactive substances such as alcohol, prescription drugs, and street drugs.

Healthcare Services & Delivery Models.label: Certified Community Behavioral Health Clinic
Healthcare Services & Delivery Models.definition: Clinics designed to provide a comprehensive range of mental health and substance use disorder services.

Legislation, Regulations & Legal Concept.label: PRWORA
Legislation, Regulations & Legal Concept.definition: The Personal Responsibility and Work Opportunity Reconciliation Act of 1996, a federal law that reformed the welfare system.
terms: Personal Responsibility and Work Opportunity Reconciliation Act of 1996
Personal Responsibility and Work Opportunity Reconciliation Act
Personal Responsibility and Work Opportunity Act

Legislation, Regulations & Legal Concept.label: Federal Register Notice
Legislation, Regulations & Legal Concept.definition: An official daily publication for rules, proposed rules, and notices of Federal agencies and organizations.

Legislation, Regulations & Legal Concept.label: Section 330
Legislation, Regulations & Legal Concept.definition: The section of the Public Health Service Act that authorizes the federal Health Center Program.
terms: Section 330 of the Public Health Service Act

Legislation, Regulations & Legal Concept.label: Public Health Service Act
Legislation, Regulations & Legal Concept.definition: A 1944 U.S. federal law giving HHS broad authority to respond to public health emergencies and conduct public health programs.

Legislation, Regulations & Legal Concept.label: Head Start Act
Legislation, Regulations & Legal Concept.definition: The federal law that authorizes the Head Start program to promote school readiness for low-income children.

Legislation, Regulations & Legal Concept.label: Administrative Procedure Act
Legislation, Regulations & Legal Concept.definition: A U.S. federal statute that governs the way in which administrative agencies of the federal government may propose and establish regulations.

Legislation, Regulations & Legal Concept.label: Public Charge Rule
Legislation, Regulations & Legal Concept.definition: An immigration ground of inadmissibility for individuals likely to become primarily dependent on the government for subsistence.

Populations & Demographics.label: Qualified Alien
Populations & Demographics.definition: A specific legal term under PRWORA for non-citizens who are eligible for certain federal public benefits.

Populations & Demographics.label: Homeless Population
Populations & Demographics.definition: Individuals and families who lack a fixed, regular, and adequate nighttime residence.

Populations & Demographics.label: Unqualified Alien
Populations & Demographics.definition: A legal term under PRWORA for non-citizens who are generally ineligible for federal public benefits.

Populations & Demographics.label: Undocumented Immigrant
Populations & Demographics.definition: A foreign-born person who does not have a legal right to be or remain in the United States.

Populations & Demographics.label: Foster Youth
Populations & Demographics.definition: Children and young adults who are in the legal custody of the state's child welfare system.

Populations & Demographics.label: Mixed-Status Family
Populations & Demographics.definition: A family whose members include people with different citizenship or immigration statuses.

Populations & Demographics.label: Lawful Permanent Resident
Populations & Demographics.definition: A non-citizen who is lawfully authorized to live permanently within the United States, also known as a green card holder.

Populations & Demographics.label: TPS Holder
Populations & Demographics.definition: An individual granted Temporary Protected Status, allowing them to live and work in the U.S. temporarily.

Populations & Demographics.label: DACA Recipient
Populations & Demographics.definition: An individual granted deferred action under the Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals program.

Populations & Demographics.label: Refugee
Populations & Demographics.definition: A person forced to flee their country because of persecution, war, or violence.
